justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Strong signals explicitly acknowledged — rollback strategy called 'well-designed and production-grade', observability called 'strong for availability and latency'. (2) Non-idempotent write + retry = guaranteed duplicates identified as the primary blocker with detailed explanation. (3) DLQ with no consumer explicitly called 'silent data loss disguised as resilience'. (4) At-least-once + non-idempotent = data quality issue under normal operation stated clearly ('not just edge cases'). (5) Specific recommendations include idempotency key (event_id) with upsert/ON CONFLICT, DLQ consumer with depth alerting. (6) Verdict is NOT READY. (7) Pippen explicitly notes strong observability cannot compensate: 'An on-call engineer cannot distinguish a healthy system from one silently producing thousands of duplicate records per minute.'

observations.text: Excellent framing of the duplicate write issue — explicitly connects retry policy, at-least-once delivery, and non-idempotent writes as a guaranteed corruption path under normal operation, not just edge cases
DLQ gap identified precisely: 'no consumer process, no alerting on queue depth, and no reprocessing mechanism — failed events enter a black hole'
Strong signals acknowledged without dismissal — deployment/rollback called 'production-grade', tracing called 'good', while clearly separating these from the blocking data correctness issues
Quantifies the impact well — '0.1% duplicate rate is 10 phantom events per second compounding forever' makes the risk concrete
Recommendations are specific and actionable: idempotency key with INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, DLQ consumer with depth/age alerting thresholds, CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY for migration safety
Identifies the observability blind spot for data correctness as a third escalation — current monitoring would not detect the very problems that make this NOT READY

Combined with at-least-once delivery semantics and retry logic (3 retries with exponential backoff), duplicate records are GUARANTEED under normal operation — not just edge cases. Eve
